explanation_used:
1. **Opening statement**: This neuron strongly responds to **mentions of specific geopolitical entities, particularly countries in Central/South Asia and the Middle East, within formal or news-reporting contexts** (such as references to Afghanistan in international investigations, military actions, or legislative bills; mentions of Tajikistan, Kazakhstan, or Iran in diplomatic or regional meetings; and names of political figures or institutions from these regions).

2. **Domain coverage**: The pattern appears predominantly in international news dispatches, diplomatic statements, and security/conflict reporting. It spans multiple languages (English, Greek, Russian, Hindi, Italian, etc.), but the content consistently involves nations like Afghanistan, Pakistan, India, Iran, and Central Asian republics. The context is often official: joint statements, troop deployments, legislative actions, or high-level political meetings.

3. **Contrast with low-activation examples**: The low-activation texts lack these specific geopolitical references. Instead, they cover entertainment, local sports, celebrity news, general technology/social issues, or domestic events in Western contexts (e.g., Ariana Grande, school competitions, TikTok bans, U.S. congressional hearings). The neuron does not fire on general news or text in foreign languages unless it centers on the detected cluster of countries and their formal affairs.

4. **Closing interpretation**: The neuron detects text segments where certain nation-states—especially Afghanistan and its neighbors—are the subject of official, often institutional, discourse. Its underlying focus is a combination of named entity recognition (specific countries) and a semantic filter for formal geopolitical or security-related events.

results.actual_text: İzmir Ekonomi Üniversitesi Rektörü Prof. Dr. Murat Aşkar, "Türkiye'de, Blackboard Learn, Blackboard Collabor
results.plain_text:
İzmir Ekonomi Üniversitesi Rektörü Prof. Dr. Murat Aşkar, "Türkiye'de, Blackboard Learn, Blackboard Collaborate ve Panopto Ders Kayıt ve İzleme Sistemi'ni kapsayan eğitim teknolojilerini aynı anda kullanan tek üniversiteyiz. Tamamı yurt dışı tecrübesine sahip akademisyenlerimiz ve son teknolojiye sahip kampüsümüzle uzaktan eğitimde de güçlü ve örnek konumdayız" dedi. 
 
BÖLGENİN İLK VAKIF ÜNİVERSİTESİ 
Ege Bölgesi'nin ilk vakıf üniversitesi olarak 14 Nisan 2001'de kurulan İzmir Ekonomi Üniversitesi, bu yıl 20'nci yaşını kutlayacağı yeni döneme, 800 akademisyen ve 9 bini aşkın öğrencisiyle online başladı. Öğrencilerin en iyi eğitimi alması için hummalı bir çalışma yürüterek yeni döneme hazırlanan İEÜ, 250 sınıfa derslerin kaydedilebileceği sistem kurdu. Uzaktan eğitime geçilince, bu altyapı yeni dönemde de etkin katkılar sağladı. Uygulamalı yapılacak bazı derslerde öğrencilerin sosyal mesafeyi korumasını sağlamak amacıyla da sıralar arasına konulmak üzere seperatörler (ayırıcı) tasarlandı. Eş zamanlı olarak derslerini işleyen ve kayıt sistemi sayesinde tekrar tekrar dinleyen, akademisyenleriyle de istedikleri zaman iletişime geçen İEÜ'lü gençler, yeni dönemde de dijital sürece hızlıca uyum sağladı. 
 
EN İYİ UYGULAYAN KURUM OLDUK 
Prof. Dr. Aşkar, "Öğrencilerimizle bir arada olamamanın burukluğu olsa da 20'nci yaşımızı kutlayacağımız yeni döneme başlamanın heyecanı içindeyiz. İzmir Ekonomi Üniversitesi olarak, Türkiye'de uzaktan eğitimi en iyi uygulayan ve sisteme en hızlı geçiş sağlayan üniversitelerin başında geliyoruz. Bizler, korona virüs öncesinde 'Akıllı Kampüs' adıyla teknoloji destekli eğitim modelini benimseyerek tüm stratejimizi bu doğrultuda oluşturduk. Teknolojik altyapıya yönelik önemli yatırımlar gerçekleştirdik, dersliklerimizi ve tüm materyalleri çağımızın teknolojik ihtiyaçlarını göz önünde bulundurarak yeniledik" dedi. 
 
UZAKTAN EĞİTİMDE GÜVEN 
Prof. Dr. Aşkar, şöyle devam etti: "Tüm bunların avantajını da bu süreçte gördük. Üniversitemizdeki 250 derslikte video kaydedici, elektronik tahta ve kameralar mevcut. Bazı bölümlerde öğrencilerimize deney ve uygulama kitleri göndereceğiz. Uygulama ve simülasyon yazılımlarının evlerdeki öğrencilerin bilgisayarlarına yüklenebilmesi için yazılım firmaları ile görüşmeler yapıyoruz. Bu çalışmalarımız sayesinde, 9 bini aşkın öğrencimiz ve akademisyenlerimizle birlikte süreci sorunsuz geçirmeye çalışıyoruz. Uzaktan eğitimde de güven veren bir kurum olmanın gururunu yaşıyoruz." ULUSLARARASI YETERLİLİĞE SAHİP GENÇLER 
İzmir Ekonomi Üniversitesi öğrencilerinin başarılarıyla fark yarattığını dile getiren Aşkar, "Türkiye'nin yanı sıra farklı ülkelerindeki firmalarda çalışan, kendi işini kuran 400'den fazla mezunumuz var. 8 dilin konuşulduğu kampüsümüzde ülkemizin ihtiyaç duyduğu nitelikli gençleri yetiştiriyoruz. Akademisyenlerimiz de Türkiye'ye örnek olacak çalışmalara imza atıyor. Örneğin, üniversitemizde sağlık çalışanlarımız için kendi kendini temizleyen özel bir maske geliştirdik. Sinema ve Dijital Medya Bölümü'nden bu yıl mezun olan öğrencimiz Çağıl Saydam, yalnız yaşayan bir kadının evde kendi kendine kurduğu diyalogları konu alan '61 Sokak No 4' filmiyle, 27'nci Uluslararası Adana Altın Koza Film Festivali'nden birincilikle döndü. Tüm bunlar özel, nitelikli bir eğitim sunduğumuzu gösteriyor" dedi. İKİ YIL İÇİNDE BAŞARI İVMESİ YAKALADIK 
Son 2 yılda ciddi aşama kaydettiklerini, bunun tercih sonuçlarına da yansıdığını söyleyen Prof. Dr. Aşkar, "Türkiye'deki vakıf üniversiteleri arasında dikkat çekici bir başarı ivmesi yakaladık. Ortalama puana göre Bilgisayar Mühendisliği bölümümüz, 2018'de 32'nci sırada yer alırken, 5'inciliğe yükseldi. Yazılım Mühendisliği bölümümüz, 10'uncu sıradayken birinciliğe yerleşti. Lojistik Yönetimi bölümümüz de birinciliğe yükseldi. Aynı şekilde halkla ilişkiler ve reklamcılık bölümümüz de 20'nci sıradan üçüncülüğe sıçradı. Türkiye'de, üniversite-şehir bütünleşmesinin ilk modeli olan Kampüsİzmir konsepti bu başarıda çok belirleyici oldu. Takip edilen, özellikle tercih edilen, yaptıklarıyla fark yaratan bir üniversite olmanın gururunu yaşıyoruz" diye konuştu. ÜÇ ASRA TANIKLIK EDEN GAZETE 
İEÜ Rektörü Prof. Dr. Murat Aşkar, Yeni Asır Gazetesi'nin 126'ncı kuruluş yıl dönümünü kutlayarak bir mesaj yayınladı. Aşkar, "1895 yılından bu yana okuyucularıyla buluşarak üç asra tanıklık eden Yeni Asır Gazetesi, Türk basınında önemli bir yere sahip. Yeni Asır Gazetesi, bugüne kadar yaptığı haberlerle Ege Bölgesi ve İzmir'in tanıtımına, gelişimine de katkılar sağladı. Yeni Asır Gazetesi'nin 126'ncı yaşını kutluyor, yayın hayatında başarılar diliyorum" ifadelerini kullandı.

results.actual_text: Chabon, David Henry Hwang, Rachel Louise Snyder, and Ayelet Waldman have also initiated legal action over copyright concerns. Moreover, OpenAI and
results.plain_text: In yet another legal confrontation, OpenAI, under the leadership of Sam Altman, and tech giant Microsoft are in the spotlight facing a fresh class-action lawsuit. The lawsuit, filed by book authors, accuses both companies of utilizing copyrighted materials without permission to build a highly profitable AI system, triggering serious allegations of intellectual property theft. Nicholas Basbanes and Nicholas Gage, non-fiction authors, have taken the initiative to file a lawsuit in Manhattan federal court, representing a group of writers who claim their copyrighted works have been consistently appropriated by Microsoft and OpenAI. The lawsuit's central accusation compares the companies to common thieves, emphasizing their alleged infringement on intellectual property. Seeking damages up to $150,000 for each work infringed, the lawsuit is a strong statement against what the authors perceive as unauthorized usage of their creations. The crux of the lawsuit revolves around OpenAI's AI system, which supposedly relies on absorbing vast amounts of written content, including books attributed to Basbanes and Gage. As of now, neither Microsoft nor OpenAI has released any official statement or comment addressing the allegations raised by the lawsuit. This legal battle adds to the string of conflicts faced by OpenAI, as previously, they encountered a lawsuit from the Authors' Guild and a group of renowned authors like Jonathan Franzen, John Grisham, George R.R. Martin, and Jodi Picoult. Additionally, authors including Michael Chabon, David Henry Hwang, Rachel Louise Snyder, and Ayelet Waldman have also initiated legal action over copyright concerns. Moreover, OpenAI and Microsoft find themselves entangled in another legal dispute involving The New York Times (NYT). The NYT has accused them of training AI models, such as ChatGPT and Copilot, on a dataset that contains copyrighted NYT articles, violating intellectual property rights. This lawsuit brings to the forefront critical questions regarding AI's role in the news industry, highlighting the ethical and legal challenges it presents. The potential implications of this legal confrontation extend to both the media and tech sectors, potentially reshaping how generative AI functions. The outcome could significantly impact the creation, consumption, and monetization of news content. results.actual_text: خرسانية من محور الفريق كمال عامر بمنطقة بولاق الدكرور بمحافظة الجيزة، وتم نقل المصاب
results.plain_text: المعاينة الأولية في حادث محور الفريق كمال عامر.. العمال كانوا أعلى الجزء المنهار قبل سقوطه  محور الفريق كمال عامر.. كشفت التحريات الأولية في حادث محور الفريق كمال عامر، والذي انهار جزء منه وتسبب في مصرع عامل وإصابة 5 آخرين، أن الأحمال فوق الكمر المنهار أدى لسقوطه. المعاينة الأولية في حادث محور الفريق كمال عامر.. العمال كانوا أعلى الجزء المنهار قبل سقوطه وقد أضافت التحريات في حادث محور الفريق كمال عامر، أن العمال اعتلوا الكمر ما أدى لسقوطه ومصرع أحدهم وإصابة الباقين، وتم نقلهم إلى المستشفى لتلقي العلاج. وفي وقت مسبق ؛ كشف الفحص الأولى أنه أثناء الأعمال الإنشائية سقطت أجزاء خرسانية من أعلى المحور بمنطقة ببولاق الدكرور. مصرع وإصابة 5 أشخاص.. بسبب سقوط جزء خرساني من محور الفريق كمال عامر ولقى عامل حتفه، بينما أصيب 5 عمال آخرين في حادث سقوط أجزاء خرسانية من محور الفريق كمال عامر بمنطقة بولاق الدكرور بمحافظة الجيزة، وتم نقل المصابين لمستشفى أم المصريين. وانتقلت الأجهزة الأمنية بمديرية أمن الجيزة إلى موقع بلاغ يفيد سقوط جزء من محور الفريق كمال عامر دائرة قسم بولاق الدكرور، وبالفحص تبين أن كمرة سقطت أثناء تركيبها، وحدوث انهيار في جزء من سور المحور. وكان قد ورد بلاغ من أهالي منطقة أبو قتادة ببولاق الدكرور يفيد سقوط جزء من محور الفريق كمال عامر من ناحية منطقة بولاق الدكرور، وعلى الفور تم الدفع بقوات الحماية المدنية وسيارات الإسعاف إلى مكان الحادث.

results.actual_text: Happy to talk about Randy Crouch, John Cooper called him one of a kind and
results.plain_text: Happy to talk about Randy Crouch, John Cooper called him one of a kind and said there’s no one else like him. How so? “He’s a psychedelic, hillbilly fiddle player from outer space,” Cooper affectionately said, coming close to repeating words used by band-mate Brad Piccolo, who once described Crouch as the original grunge hillbilly fiddle player from outer space. Continuing, Cooper said Crouch is just a really unique guy in the way that he thinks and the way that he is. “My quote on Randy is this: Randy Crouch doesn’t play music; Randy Crouch is music. I believe that’s true. He thinks music. He is music. That’s who that guy is — and he has a heart of gold.” Other musicians love Crouch because he is so musical, according to Cooper, who is pleased Crouch has been selected for induction into the Oklahoma Music Hall of Fame. The Oklahoma Music Hall of Fame’s Class of 2022 will be honored during a Sunday, Feb. 27 ceremony at Cain’s Ballroom. Crouch is going in alongside Mike McClure and Don White. Also in the class is Don Byas, who will be honored posthumously. Cooper’s observation — “Randy Crouch doesn’t play music; Randy Crouch is music” — was shared with Crouch’s sister and sometimes band-mate, Lisa Perry. “It’s true,” she said. “From very early on, he pretty much could play anything he picked up. Our grandfather, who happened to be a cook on the Santa Fe Trail, he was like a cowboy. ... He taught Randy how to play the fiddle one night. Randy asked him if he could help him that evening and, by the end of the night, he could play it. He’s so versatile ... I can’t think of anything that Randy ever had in his hand that he couldn’t play and become incredibly accomplished on.” Crouch appreciates that he is being honored — “I’m so grateful to have a chance to be a part of something so great as the Oklahoma Music Hall of Fame” — but he’s a better music-maker than self-promoter. He said he feels “not worthy.” He called the other inductees “heroes of mine, each one of them, for what they have done.” Can he play anything? “No. No. No,” Crouch said during a phone interview from his home. “I can probably break anything. We’ve got a lot of broken instruments around here. I’m always thinking maybe it can be fixed.” Who’s the world’s best rock and roll fiddler? Crouch said that’s Byron Berline, who played with the Rolling Stones, the Flying Burrito Brothers and others. Berline died last year. Crouch said there are several people lost recently that he wishes could be around for the hall of fame ceremony. Crouch indicated he will use his platform at the ceremony to offer thank yous and apologies. “It will be really fun getting a chance to see people that I haven’t seen in a while,” he said, adding that he is looking forward to the jam session at the ceremony. “I’ve got to pick three songs. I think that’s the hardest part, just thinking about that, I have had to re-learn several of the songs that I needed to practice on anyway,” he said. A singer, multi-instrumentalist and songwriter credited with influencing music artists in multiple genres, Crouch was raised in northwest Texas as the son of a Methodist preacher. He played in a high school band, The Wipeouts, in Crosbyton, Texas. “Mom would come out and chaperone the dances,” he said. “A lot of people in town thought dancing was a sin and all that.” Crouch recalled that it was fun playing rock and roll back then and trying to learn songs he heard on the radio. “To me, I didn’t think there was any difference between Roger Miller and the Beatles,” he said. “They were both writing great songs. I didn’t think about one thing being country and one thing being rock.” Crouch said he was 17 when he came to Tulsa and heard all the great bands here. That, he said, was his musical education. He never saw Bob Wills with the Texas Playboys, but he saw the band minus Wills and learned a lot about how the group blended instruments and played many styles of music. Crouch believes Cain’s Ballroom, which Wills transformed into the Carnegie Hall of western swing music, is the perfect place for the hall of fame ceremony because Wills was such a good collector of people and musical ideas. At one point, Crouch and his sister were in house bands at Cain’s Ballroom and another Tulsa club, the Nine of Cups, according to Perry. A past Tulsa World story described Crouch this way: “Back in the late ’70s and early ’80s, red dirt music virtuoso Randy Crouch pretty well took the then-popular idea of the ‘cosmic cowboy’ and rocketed with it into a psychedelically-infused mirror universe.” The story said Crouch played music that lay somewhere between Wills and Jimi Hendrix. Crouch’s band memberships include an affiliation with the Red Dirt Rangers, inducted into the Oklahoma Music Hall of Fame in 2017. “With Randy, for the last 25 years, our agreement with him was we want you to play every show you can, but if you can’t make it, no problem,” Cooper said. “So it’s kind of a loose (arrangement). We tell him about the gigs and, if he can make it, great. And if he can’t make it, that’s OK, too. He’s always welcome on stage with us. Over the past 20 years, he made 80% of the shows. He’s his own thing, no doubt about it, but we also consider him a Red Dirt Ranger.” Crouch predominantly plays the fiddle, guitar and steel guitar. Cooper said he has seen Crouch play them all “basically at the same time.” In addition to those instruments, Crouch marches to the beat of his own drum. He lives in a dome he designed and built in Teresita, near Spring Creek and the Illinois River in Cherokee County. “I lived out here for over 30 years without electric and then finally got electric here recently,” he said. Power was previously supplied by way of solar panels, generator and innovative use of car batteries. “Now it’s pretty nice to have electric,” he said. “If it goes out, we know how to live without it.” Perry said her brother is a great character and, early on, he was so enigmatic that people were interested in flocking to him. She is proud of her brother, and not just because he’s a musician’s musician and a hall of famer. She described him as a kind and loving soul. Cooper said Crouch has never met a stranger. “He’s just Randy — just so unique and special and just a beloved man,” Cooper said. “Everybody that knows him loves him, especially in music circles. His playing is just one of a kind. I have heard him play everything from Hendrix to Vivaldi. He does ‘William Tell Overture’ like nobody’s business.”

results.actual_text: WA Police have backed in an officer who was filmed punching a man multiple times while being
results.plain_text: WA Police have backed in an officer who was filmed punching a man multiple times while being pinned to the ground in Northbridge on New Year’s Eve. WATCH THE ARREST FOOTAGE IN THE VIDEO PLAYER ABOVE Vision of the arrest, outside the Brass Monkey just after the clock struck midnight, emerged online — showing one police officer throwing several punches at a 25-year-old suspect on his side. Three officers in total can be seen pinning the man to the ground as other partygoers record him receiving multiple across his body — some near his head. On Tuesday, Police Commissioner Col Blanch said the officer had acted within WA Police’s training, in order to arrest the man. “The law allows police to use force when a person is not complying with an arrest. Using force can sometimes look confronting on video but often is reasonable in the circumstances,” Commissioner Blanch. “When someone is not complying during an arrest, Officers are trained to employ distraction techniques, such as strikes to the body.” Commissioner Blanch said the nearby crowd was becoming increasingly agitated, and officers even had a glass thrown from a pub balcony above them at one point. “In this instance, the alleged offender appeared to be resisting and the Officer was unable to get the handcuffs on. In these circumstances, Officers are trained to create a distraction in order to complete the arrest,” he said. “After watching the footage of the arrest personally, I want to make it clear this Officer has, in my view, acted within his training and has my support. We are transparent in our actions and will investigate any complaint of excessive force with oversight of the CCC. “He was one of thousands of my Officers who put keeping our community safe ahead of their own New Year’s Eve and Christmas celebrations with family and friends. “It’s a very difficult job in these circumstances and it’s important we recognise the challenges of managing large crowds where many people are affected by alcohol.” The 25-year-old man — from Kenwick — has been handed one count of disorderly behaviour in a public place and one count of obstructing public officers. He is due to face Perth Magistrates Court on January 31.
